skill.name: fastqc-report-interpreter
skill.description: Use when analyzing FASTQC quality reports from sequencing data, identifying quality issues in NGS datasets, or troubleshooting sequencing problems. Interprets quality metrics and provides actionable recommendations for RNA-seq, DNA-seq, and ChIP-seq data.
skill.summary: Interprets FASTQC quality metrics for NGS sequencing data and provides actionable recommendations for RNA-seq, DNA-seq, and ChIP-seq experiments.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 67 static analysis findings across 3 files (689 lines). All findings are false positives: 35 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hits match on bioinformatics quality threshold dictionary keys ('good', 'warning', 'fail') in scripts/main.py with no actual cryptographic operations. 30 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' hits are GitHub-Flavored Markdown code formatting backticks in SKILL.md documentation. 3 'system reconnaissance' hits are standard workflow discipline text. 2 'hardcoded URL' hits reference a legitimate GitHub source repository. The skill is a benign FASTQC report interpreter with no command injection, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior. It reads JSON input files and prints human-readable quality assessments.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub Source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a reference URL to the source GitHub repository. This is a legitimate source attribution and does not represent a security risk. The skill makes no runtime network connections.

content.user_title: Interpret FASTQC Quality Reports for NGS Sequencing Data
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics researchers need to interpret complex FASTQC quality reports before analyzing sequencing data. This skill translates technical quality metrics into plain-language assessments and provides targeted recommendations for improving data quality.
actual_capabilities: Parse FASTQC reports and interpret per-base quality, per-sequence quality, GC content, adapter content, and duplication levels
Generate plain-language explanations for each quality metric with pass, warning, or fail status
Provide targeted recommendations for quality issues such as trimming parameters and contamination checks
Apply application-specific quality thresholds for RNA-seq, DNA-seq, and ChIP-seq experiments
Diagnose common sequencing problems including adapter contamination, PCR duplication, and quality degradation
limitations: Does not perform FASTQC analysis - requires pre-generated FASTQC reports as input
Cannot access raw sequencing data for deeper quality investigation
Interpretation is based on simplified threshold rules and may not cover all specialized protocols
Does not provide statistical comparisons between samples or advanced visualization

use_cases.title: Quality Check for RNA-seq Experiment
use_cases.description: Assess the quality of multiple RNA-seq samples before proceeding with differential expression analysis using appropriate RNA-seq quality thresholds.

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting Failed Sequencing Run
use_cases.description: Identify why a sequencing run produced low-quality data and determine corrective actions such as trimming, adapter removal, or protocol adjustments.

use_cases.title: FASTQC Report Review for Publication
use_cases.description: Verify that sequencing data quality metrics meet publication standards and produce documentation suitable for a methods section or data availability statement.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single-Sample Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You have one FASTQC report and want a quick quality overview
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a FASTQC report from my sequencing run. Please analyze the quality metrics and tell me if the data is good enough for downstream analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Application-Specific Quality Check
prompt_templates.scenario: You need quality thresholds specific to your experiment type
prompt_templates.prompt: Please analyze this FASTQC report for my RNA-seq experiment. Apply RNA-seq specific thresholds and tell me if I should proceed with quantification.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis with Summary Table
prompt_templates.scenario: You have multiple samples and need a comparative summary
prompt_templates.prompt: I have FASTQC reports from 12 samples in a batch. Please analyze all reports, identify which samples have quality issues, and provide a summary table with pass, warning, or fail status for each metric across all samples.

prompt_templates.title: Full Diagnostic Workflow with Recommendations
prompt_templates.scenario: You need comprehensive troubleshooting with remediation steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete diagnostic analysis of my FASTQC report. For each module that fails, explain the likely cause such as sequencing chemistry issue, library prep problem, or bioinformatics artifact, and provide step-by-step remediation recommendations.

output_examples.input: What is the quality of my sequencing data? Here is the FASTQC report.
output: Per Base Quality: GOOD (mean Q-score: 32 across all cycles)
Per Sequence Quality: GOOD (mean quality score peaks at Q31)
GC Content: WARNING (8% deviation from expected distribution)
- GC content shows a slight deviation. This is common in RNA-seq data and should not significantly impact quantification.
- High duplication detected. Consider using a deduplication tool before variant calling. For future experiments, reduce PCR cycles during library preparation.

output_examples.input: My ChIP-seq FASTQC report shows adapter contamination. What should I do?
output: Severity: WARNING (8% adapter content detected across reads)

Run adapter trimming with cutadapt or Trimmomatic using the detected adapter sequences. For ChIP-seq data, even low-level adapter contamination can affect peak calling sensitivity. After trimming, re-run FASTQC to confirm the adapters have been removed before proceeding with alignment.

output_examples.input: Compare these two sequencing runs and tell me which one is better for variant calling.
output: Run A: PASS - All metrics within acceptable ranges. Mean Q-score of 34 across all cycles. Duplication at 15% indicates good library complexity. Suitable for all downstream applications including variant calling.

Run B: FAIL - Per base quality drops below Q20 after cycle 100. High duplication at 55% and 12% adapter contamination detected. Recommended actions: quality trim the last 50 bases, run adapter removal, and deduplicate before use.

Recommendation: Use Run A for primary variant calling analysis. Run B may be salvageable for less sensitive applications after quality processing, but is not recommended for clinical-grade variant detection.

best_practices: Always use the most recent FASTQC report generated after any quality trimming or filtering steps to verify that corrections were effective
Check application-specific thresholds: RNA-seq tolerates higher duplication levels due to transcript abundance, while DNA-seq for variant calling requires stricter quality standards
Review all FASTQC modules together rather than focusing on individual metrics, as quality issues often correlate across modules and provide a more complete picture
anti_patterns: Relying on a single quality metric to determine if data is usable - always consider the full set of FASTQC modules together for a complete assessment
Applying the same quality thresholds to all experiment types without adjusting for application-specific requirements such as RNA-seq versus DNA-seq
Proceeding with analysis on data that fails per-base quality without first investigating and addressing the root cause of the quality degradation

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill works with FASTQC reports. You can provide the HTML report content or the structured quality data from a FASTQC analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill fix low-quality sequencing data?
faq.answer: No. The skill identifies quality issues and provides recommendations, but you must perform the suggested corrections using tools like cutadapt, Trimmomatic, or fastp.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for single-cell sequencing data?
faq.answer: The standard thresholds apply, but single-cell data has additional quality considerations like barcode diversity and UMI analysis that this skill does not address.

faq.question: What is the difference between PASS, WARNING, and FAIL status?
faq.answer: PASS means the metric meets quality standards. WARNING means the metric is borderline and should be reviewed. FAIL means the metric requires corrective action before proceeding with downstream analysis.

faq.question: Can I process multiple FASTQC reports at once?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide multiple reports in a single request and the skill will summarize the quality status across all samples.

faq.question: Does this skill support long-read sequencing platforms like PacBio or Oxford Nanopore?
faq.answer: The standard FASTQC metrics and thresholds are designed for short-read Illumina data. Long-read platforms have different quality characteristics that this skill does not fully address.
